summary: 6 wins, 1 loss, 8 no-picks (7 PENDING_SP_DATA plus the postponed MIL@PIT) from 15 matchups — an 85.7% directional hit rate on the 7 scored games. The headline result is CLE@MIA: Claude rescued a HARD-CAP-10-zeroed pre-cap PLATINUM (all four grading dimensions affirming) back to full stake using the 6/12 ARI@CIN exception rule, and CLE won 3-2 — a second clean validation of that rescue heuristic. Three more CAP-10-suppressed SKIPs (PHI@DET, NYY@WSH, BOS@NYM) also won unstaked, an outlier against the bucket's cited 31.2% historical hit rate. HARD CAP 1 correctly flagged the slate's only loss (ATL@STL, Sale DIAMOND at -11.61pp edge), while HARD CAP 7 broke its 0-for-7 backtest streak on KC@BAL.

PHI @ DET.headline: DET routed PHI 10-2; a thin pre-cap score=1 SKIP (PQI against, CAP 10 suppressed) still won outright.
PHI @ DET.hypothesis: DET was a thin pre-cap score=1 pick (only F3_swing_take_gap=1490.1 and Stage agreement affirming), with PQI actually running Δ=-9.6 against the pick and pick_prob=0.532 tripping HARD CAP 10 — appropriately suppressed to SKIP. DET blew out PHI 10-2 anyway, and Aaron Nola's (PHI) archive-noted 3-for-3 track record snapped. The 8-run margin suggests a team-quality gap (DET's lineup depth over Flaherty's start) the model's signals didn't fully capture, distinct from clean variance — though no batter-level data was populated to confirm.
PHI @ DET.signals_to_recheck: [HARD CAP 10] bottom-bucket suppression (pick_prob=0.532) correctly withheld stake but the pick won 10-2 — one of three pure CAP-10 wins today, see patterns_observed; PQI Δ=-9.6 against pick did not predict the blowout margin.

MIL @ PIT.headline: Game postponed; no result to grade.
MIL @ PIT.hypothesis: MIL@PIT was postponed per statsapi. The D-grade SKIP pick (Ashcraft xERA 1.51 against MIL, PQI Δ+15.9 against) could not be evaluated against an actual outcome. No process or variance judgment is possible.
MIL @ PIT.signals_to_recheck: N/A — game postponed; revisit signal stack (SP xERA against, PQI against) when the game is rescheduled and played.

NYY @ WSH.headline: NYY won 5-3; a CAP-10-suppressed PLATINUM pick paid off despite a thin pre-cap score=2 stack.
NYY @ WSH.hypothesis: NYY's pre-cap PLATINUM reached only score=2 (F3_swing_take_gap=3222.2 and F5_bullpen_gap=+1.60 affirming, no PQI or SP-xERA dimension scored) before pick_prob=0.533 tripped HARD CAP 10, correctly suppressing to SKIP given the thin stack. NYY won 5-3 regardless. Ryan Weathers (WSH) was flagged in the prior claude_picks entry as a 'developing-pitcher SP-xERA misfire' risk, but no misfire was needed — NYY won on its own lineup/bullpen signals.
NYY @ WSH.signals_to_recheck: [HARD CAP 10] bottom-bucket suppression (pick_prob=0.533) — second of three pure CAP-10 wins today (with PHI@DET, BOS@NYM); F3_swing_take_gap=3222.2 and F5_bullpen_gap=+1.60 both directionally validated.

KC @ BAL.headline: BAL won 5-3; HARD CAP 7's previously perfect 0-for-7 backtest broke on a marginal bullpen-gap flag.
KC @ BAL.hypothesis: BAL's pre-cap B+ (F-signal, PQI confirms Δ+9.5, Stage agreement, score=3) was capped to B- by HARD CAP 7 (pick-side bullpen disadvantage, hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap=+0.018), and Claude DOWNGRADED to SKIP citing the cap's 0-for-7 backtest record. BAL won 5-3 anyway — the first break in that streak. The underlying gap (+0.018) sits well under the 0.04 threshold the signal vocabulary calls 'meaningful,' suggesting CAP 7 fired on an immaterial bullpen difference this time.
KC @ BAL.signals_to_recheck: [HARD CAP 7] pick-side bullpen disadvantage (hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap=+0.018, below the 0.04 'meaningful' threshold) — first loss to the cap's 0-for-7 backtest record; BAL won 5-3 despite the flag.

CHC @ CIN.headline: CIN shut out CHC 4-0; no model pick existed (Hunter Greene below the 100-pitch Statcast gate).
CHC @ CIN.hypothesis: Hunter Greene (CIN) was below the 100-pitch Statcast xERA-scoring minimum in the diag CSV; live statsapi had already confirmed both Greene and Imanaga (CHC) as starters, so the pipeline should score this game on its next run. CIN won 4-0. No directional call was possible today.
CHC @ CIN.signals_to_recheck: PENDING_SP_DATA (Greene pitch-count gate) — re-score once the threshold clears; Imanaga's (CHC) archive track record should be weighed against today's shutout loss once graded.

SEA @ TB.headline: TB won 7-2; a clean C-grade SKIP in an unresolved Stage 1/2 disagreement zone still landed on the right side.
SEA @ TB.hypothesis: TB was a clean C-grade SKIP: PQI ran Δ=-3.6 against the pick and Stage 1/2 disagreed meaningfully (f5_full_delta=0.1797, above the 0.12 disagreement trigger) — a zone the archive has flip-flopped on all season with no stable rule. TB won 7-2, a comfortable margin favoring the Stage 2/full-game direction here, but one result doesn't resolve the standing ambiguity.
SEA @ TB.signals_to_recheck: f5_full_delta=0.1797 (Stage 1/2 disagreement zone remains unresolved — today favored the pick/Stage-2 direction); PQI Δ=-3.6 against pick did not predict the 5-run TB margin.

CLE @ MIA.headline: CLE won 3-2 on the road; Claude's HARD-CAP-10 rescue (pre-cap score=5, all four dimensions affirming) paid off for a second consecutive validation of the 6/12 exception rule.
CLE @ MIA.hypothesis: CLE's pre-cap A-grade (score=5) had every dimension affirm: F-signal (F1_xera_gap=0.88*, F3=4043.1, F5_bullpen=+0.91), SP xERA +0.56 agreeing, PQI confirms Δ-6.5, and Stage 1/2 agreement (Δ=0.00) — the same four-dimension-affirm profile the 6/12 ARI@CIN postgame flagged as a legitimate exception to HARD CAP 10's bottom-bucket suppression. Claude applied that exception and confirmed at the full pre-cap PLATINUM level instead of accepting the capped SKIP. CLE won 3-2 over Sandy Alcantara (MIA), whose archive-tracked 3-1 record was a real caution but was outweighed by the complete signal affirmation. This is a genuine process win.
CLE @ MIA.signals_to_recheck: [HARD CAP 10] exception now validated twice (6/12 ARI@CIN, 7/10 CLE@MIA) — pre-cap score=5 with F-signal + SP-xERA + PQI + Stage-agreement all affirming simultaneously looks like a durable rescue condition worth formalizing in the pipeline; F1_xera_gap=0.88* (starred, thin sample) was corroborated by F3=4043.1, consistent with the 'F1* needs secondary confirmation' rule.

BOS @ NYM.headline: BOS won 6-2; a third CAP-10-suppressed pick to win outright today despite PQI running against it.
BOS @ NYM.hypothesis: BOS's pre-cap score=1 was thin — PQI ran Δ=+5.8 against the pick, leaving only F3 (6136.4) and Stage agreement affirming, well short of the CLE@MIA four-dimension bar. HARD CAP 10 correctly suppressed the stake (pick_prob=0.533). BOS won 6-2 regardless, extending today's CAP-10 pure-suppression win streak. Nolan McLean's (NYM) archive-noted dominant start did not repeat; McLean took the loss.
BOS @ NYM.signals_to_recheck: [HARD CAP 10] bottom-bucket suppression (pick_prob=0.533) — correctly withheld stake, but the pick won 6-2, the third such case today (see patterns_observed); PQI Δ=+5.8 against pick also failed to predict the loser.

ATL @ STL.headline: STL edged ATL 2-1; HARD CAP 1's negative-edge suppression correctly called the slate's only loss despite an elite raw signal stack.
ATL @ STL.hypothesis: ATL/Sale carried the strongest raw signal stack of the slate (SP xERA +2.12 agreeing, PQI confirms Δ-37.6, F-signal, Stage agreement — pre-cap score=4), but a real -11.61pp negative edge tripped HARD CAP 1, which the archive treats as non-negotiable even on elite SP-edge stacks (the 6/4 lesson: upgrading past CAP 1 on an 'ace start' thesis also lost, 7-2). CAP 10 then zeroed the residual. ATL lost 1-2 — the market's negative edge correctly anticipated the loser despite Sale's individual quality. Clean process success.
ATL @ STL.signals_to_recheck: [HARD CAP 1] negative_edge_GOLD (-11.61pp) correctly predicted the slate's only loss despite an elite pre-cap signal stack — second Sale/ATL confirmation of CAP 1's non-negotiable status (6/4, 7/10).

OAK @ CHW.headline: CHW blew out the Athletics 14-1; no model pick existed (OAK's SP unannounced in the diag CSV).
OAK @ CHW.hypothesis: OAK's probable SP (Jacob Lopez) was unannounced in the diag CSV; live statsapi had already confirmed Lopez vs Sean Burke, so the pipeline should pick this up next run. Athletics were blown out 14-1. No directional call was possible today.
OAK @ CHW.signals_to_recheck: PENDING_SP_DATA (OAK SP unannounced in CSV, confirmed at statsapi runtime) — re-score once the pipeline catches up.

HOU @ TEX.hypothesis: TEX's probable SP (Cal Quantrill) was unannounced in the CSV; statsapi confirmed Quantrill vs Hunter Brown. TEX won 7-3. No pick was graded.
HOU @ TEX.signals_to_recheck: PENDING_SP_DATA (TEX SP unannounced) — re-score once the pipeline catches up.

LAA @ MIN.hypothesis: LAA's probable SP (Grayson Rodriguez) was unannounced in the CSV; statsapi confirmed Rodriguez vs Zebby Matthews. LAA won 4-3. No pick was graded.
LAA @ MIN.signals_to_recheck: PENDING_SP_DATA (LAA SP unannounced) — re-score once the pipeline catches up.

TOR @ SD.headline: TOR won 5-3; no model pick existed (TOR's SP unannounced in the diag CSV).
TOR @ SD.hypothesis: TOR's probable SP (Shane Bieber) was unannounced in the CSV; statsapi confirmed Bieber vs JP Sears. TOR won 5-3. No pick was graded.
TOR @ SD.signals_to_recheck: PENDING_SP_DATA (TOR SP unannounced) — re-score once the pipeline catches up.

ARI @ LAD.headline: ARI won convincingly 9-3; no model pick existed (ARI's SP unannounced in the diag CSV), and Ohtani (LAD) took the loss.
ARI @ LAD.hypothesis: ARI's probable SP (Eduardo Rodriguez) was unannounced in the CSV; statsapi confirmed E. Rodriguez vs Shohei Ohtani (LAD). Despite the archive's generally positive Ohtani-start track record, ARI won 9-3 and Ohtani took the loss. No pick was graded, but this is a second Ohtani loss in the tracked sample (6/10, 7/10).
ARI @ LAD.signals_to_recheck: PENDING_SP_DATA (ARI SP unannounced) — re-score once the pipeline catches up; Ohtani's start résumé now includes a second tracked loss, worth revisiting the 'generally positive' framing.

COL @ SF.headline: COL won 4-3 in a one-run game; no model pick existed (Robbie Ray below the 100-pitch Statcast gate).
COL @ SF.hypothesis: SF's probable SP (Robbie Ray) was below the 100-pitch Statcast xERA-scoring minimum; statsapi confirmed Ray vs Tanner Gordon (COL). COL won 4-3. No pick was graded.
COL @ SF.signals_to_recheck: PENDING_SP_DATA (Ray pitch-count gate) — re-score once the threshold clears.

patterns_observed: HARD CAP 10 bottom-bucket suppression (pick_prob 0.50-0.55, no CONFIRM) fired cleanly on 4 games today (PHI@DET, NYY@WSH, BOS@NYM, and CLE@MIA before Claude's rescue) — every one of them won (DET 10-2, NYY 5-3, BOS 6-2, CLE 3-2), sharply above the archive's cited 31.2% non-CONFIRM hit rate for this bucket. This doesn't override CAP 10 on one slate, but the bucket's hit rate should be tracked over the next 10-15 picks before treating 31.2% as settled.

CLE@MIA validates the HARD CAP 10 rescue exception (pre-cap score=5, all four dimensions — F-signal, SP-xERA, PQI, Stage agreement — affirming) for a second time (6/12 ARI@CIN, 7/10 CLE@MIA), both wins. Recommend formalizing this as a standing exception in the grading pipeline rather than a per-game Claude judgment call.

HARD CAP 7 (pick-side bullpen disadvantage) broke its 0-for-7 backtest streak today: BAL was downgraded on a hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap of only +0.018 — well under the 0.04 threshold the signal vocabulary calls 'meaningful' — and won 5-3 anyway. Consider requiring |gap| > 0.04 to fire CAP 7 rather than any gap >= 0.

HARD CAP 1 (negative-edge GOLD/DIAMOND) again correctly caught the slate's only loss — ATL/Sale's elite pre-cap signal stack (SP xERA +2.12, PQI confirms) was overridden by a real -11.61pp edge, and ATL lost 1-2, echoing the 6/4 Sale/ATL precedent. This remains the single most validated cap in the archive.
